meta.title: WorkflowAI Pro — Enterprise AI Governance Platform Specification (2026–2030)
meta.subtitle: Architecture, Product Requirements, Safety & Governance, AGI/ASI Preparedness, and Implementation Strategy for Fortune 500 Enterprises
meta.classification: CONFIDENTIAL — Platform Engineering / CAIO / CISO / CDO / Legal
meta.owner: Chief AI Officer + Head of Platform Engineering
audience: CAIO, CIO, CISO, CDO, Chief Privacy Officer
Treaty observers (EU AI Office, UK AISI, US AISI)
meta.hostingModel: Customer-hosted (VPC) or dedicated SaaS; BYO-KMS; hybrid-cloud ready
keyDifferentiators: Integrated Cognitive Orchestrator + Sentinel compliance engine
AGI/ASI-ready safety scaffolding (containment simulations, PID alignment)
Cryptographically signed audit trails with Merkle-DAG evidence
Active learning loop with signed human feedback
standardsAlignment: NIST AI RMF 1.0 + Generative AI Profile
SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA (optional profile), FedRAMP Moderate (targeted 2028)

executiveSummary.thesis: WorkflowAI Pro is the governance-native enterprise AI platform that fuses prompt engineering, agent orchestration, model governance, compliance automation, and AGI/ASI safety scaffolding into a single auditable metabolism. It is designed for Fortune 500 enterprises operating under converging regulatory regimes (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, SR 11-7) while preparing for frontier-capable systems through 2030.
coreCapabilities: Prompt lifecycle: history, templates, variable linking, test area, categories, import/export
Agent simulation & canary deployment with SLO-gated promotion
Cognitive Orchestrator dashboard: multi-agent DAGs, live telemetry, alignment PID
Sentinel compliance engine: policy-as-code (OPA/Rego), automated reporting, evidence bundles
Containment-breach simulation suite (CB-01…CB-10) mapped to MITRE ATLAS
Signed active-learning loop (Ed25519) feeding Gemini via backend proxy
Enhanced RBAC with attribute-based access control (ABAC) overlays
Task-dependency DAG visualisation (D3.js) with causal lineage
executiveSummary.governanceThesis: Governance is not an overlay; it is the control plane. Every prompt, variable, template, agent, deployment, and model inference emits tamper-evident evidence, is filtered by policy-as-code at CI/CD and runtime, and is reconcilable against a pre-declared alignment specification via PID-based tuning with auditable setpoints.
businessOutcomes: 50–70% reduction in audit preparation effort (evidence bundles pre-assembled)
3× faster safe rollout of new agents via canary + simulation gates
Quantifiable Pillar-2 capital-impact reduction for regulated AI uses
Board-grade explainability via Cognitive Orchestrator + Sentinel reports

m1_architecture.title: Platform Architecture — 7-Layer Reference
m1_architecture.summary: WorkflowAI Pro is built as seven cooperating layers: Presentation, API Gateway, Orchestration, Model & Tool Plane, Policy & Evidence Plane, Data Plane, and Observability/SRE.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.purpose: React 18 + TypeScript SPA with Vite; Tailwind + shadcn/ui; D3.js for DAG visualisation; Monaco editor for prompt authoring.
components: Prompt Studio (history, templates, variable linking, test area)
layers.stateManagement: TanStack Query + Zustand + URL-state via React Router v6
layers.accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA; keyboard-first; screen-reader landmarks; high-contrast theme

layers.name: API Gateway
layers.purpose: Backend-routed entry point; authentication, rate-limiting, request shaping, Zod validation.
components: Zod schema validators applied at the edge
Centralized error handler with RFC 7807 Problem Details
layers.runtime: Node.js 22 (Fastify) with tRPC for internal services
layers.secrets: Backend-only; no Gemini/OpenAI keys in frontend; KMS-sealed envelopes

layers.name: Orchestration
layers.purpose: Cognitive Orchestrator — multi-agent DAG scheduler with guardrails, retries, and kill-switch integration.
components: Policy hook (OPA sidecar) evaluated before each step

layers.name: Model & Tool Plane
layers.purpose: Uniform adapter for Gemini, Claude, GPT, OSS models + tool calls; deterministic replay via seeded inputs.

layers.name: Policy & Evidence Plane
layers.purpose: Policy-as-code gate + tamper-evident evidence storage (Merkle-DAG, Ed25519 signatures).
components: Evidence ledger (append-only Postgres + S3 Object Lock)

layers.name: Data Plane
layers.purpose: Tenant-isolated storage for prompts, templates, runs, feedback, embeddings.
components: Postgres 16 with row-level security (RLS) per tenant
S3 (or equivalent) with Object Lock for immutable artefacts
Redis for ephemeral run state & locks

layers.name: Observability & SRE
layers.purpose: Operational visibility, SLOs, chaos, kill-switch validation.

nfrs.name: Data Residency
nfrs.target: Region-pinned; US, EU, UK, SG, AU, JP

topologies.name: Dedicated SaaS
topologies.description: Single-tenant control plane + data plane in vendor-managed VPC

topologies.name: Customer VPC
topologies.description: Terraform-deployed into customer AWS/Azure/GCP with BYO-KMS

topologies.name: Hybrid Air-Gap
topologies.description: Control plane in SaaS, data plane on-premise; signed policy bundle pull

topologies.name: Regulated Regional
topologies.description: Region-pinned with data-sovereignty attestations (EU, UK, SG)

m2_strategy.summary: Four-horizon strategy aligning AI capability expansion with governance maturity and AGI-readiness.

outcomes: EU AI Act high-risk inventory complete
EAIP v1 interop with 3+ partner platforms

m3_agi.title: AGI/ASI Governance, Safety & Communication Frameworks
m3_agi.summary: Layered safety, containment, and communication architecture preparing WorkflowAI Pro for frontier-capable systems.

m4_reports.title: Formal AI Safety & Global Governance Technical Reports
m4_reports.summary: Library of standard technical reports produced by the platform, aligned to regulatory and treaty expectations.

sections.title: Report Generation Pipeline
pipeline: Evidence collector pulls signed artefacts from ledger
Sentinel policy checks block on missing mandatory fields
PDF pipeline styles output (theme, headers, footers, watermark)
Signer (Ed25519) attaches detached signature + Merkle proof
Delivery: secure portal download + optional treaty API push

m5_prompt.title: Prompt Lifecycle Features — Product Requirements
m5_prompt.summary: End-to-end prompt engineering UX: history, templates, variable linking, test area, categories, import/export.

sections.title: Prompt History
requirements: Immutable run ledger keyed by (tenantId, promptId, runId)
Diff view between any two runs (prompt text + variables + output)
Tagging: favourite, flagged, baseline, production
Searchable by author, tag, model, outcome, regex
Export selected runs to JSONL or PDF dossier
Retention policy: configurable 30d/90d/7y with legal-hold override

sections.title: Prompt Templates
requirements: First-class entity with semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
Variables declared with type (string, number, enum, file, vectorRef)
Guardrail directives (refuse-to-answer lists, PII masks)
Linked test cases (golden outputs for regression)
Approval workflow: draft → review → approved → retired
Lineage to child prompts derived from this template

sections.title: Variable Linking UI
requirements: Drag-and-drop variable binding from data sources
Autocomplete with provenance badges (source, freshness, classification)
Type validation at bind-time (Zod)
Sensitive variables flagged with classification (PII, PHI, PCI, EXPORT)
Preview pane rendering resolved prompt with highlighted substitutions
Broken-link detector with one-click repair suggestions

sections.title: Test Prompt Area
requirements: Side-by-side model comparison (up to 4)
Deterministic seed support
Token/cost budget sliders
Assertions DSL (e.g. 'contains', 'json.schema', 'semSim>=0.85')
Capture-to-template button (promotes input/output to golden case)
Inline Sentinel checks (bias, PII leak, jailbreak signals)

sections.title: Template Export / Import & Categories
requirements: Export format: EAIP-TPX v1 (JSON + detached signature)
Categories taxonomy (industry, function, risk-tier, language)
Bulk import with validation report
Schema migrations on import with automatic upgrades
Marketplace-ready metadata (author, licence, support)
Compatibility matrix (model families supported)

m6_agents.title: Agent Simulation, Canary Deployment, EAIP Interop & Containment Simulations
m6_agents.summary: Safe rollout controls and interoperability for multi-agent systems.

sections.title: Agent Simulation
capabilities: Deterministic replay of past traffic against new agent version
Synthetic scenario injection (library + generator)
Counterfactual evaluation (holding user intent fixed, varying agent)
Safety evals: jailbreak, goal-misgeneralisation, deceptive alignment probes
Economic cost model: token + tool-call + human-review projections

sections.title: Canary Deployment
capabilities: Percentage + cohort-based routing (geo, persona, risk-tier)
SLO-gated auto-promote / auto-rollback
Dual-run comparison (shadow + primary)
Kill-switch integration on SLO/metric breach
Automatic evidence capture of canary decisions
promotionCriteria: Zero P1 incidents in 7-day canary window

sections.title: EAIP Interoperability (Enterprise AI Interchange Profile)
capabilities: Portable template (EAIP-TPX) and evaluation bundle (EAIP-EVB) formats
Cross-platform run manifest (EAIP-RMX) with signed Merkle root
OAuth 2.1 federated identity with delegated tool scopes
Mutual attestation between WorkflowAI Pro and partner platforms
Equivalence certificate generation (model + policy pair)

sections.title: Containment Breach Simulation Suite
sections.summary: Ten simulated scenarios (CB-01–CB-10) mapped to MITRE ATLAS; scheduled + ad-hoc execution.

scenarios.name: Goal misgeneralisation leading to unsafe action

m7_orchestrator.title: Cognitive Orchestrator & Sentinel Compliance Engine
m7_orchestrator.summary: The operational brain (orchestration) and the governance conscience (compliance) of WorkflowAI Pro.

panels.name: Live DAG
panels.desc: Active agent graphs with status, latency, cost, safety signals

panels.name: Alignment PID
panels.desc: Setpoint vs. measured alignment with error, integral, derivative trails

panels.name: Safety Signals
panels.desc: Refusal quality, jailbreak attempts, policy violations

panels.name: Canary Status
panels.desc: Per-agent canary share, health, promotion recommendations

panels.name: Evidence Flow
panels.desc: Events/sec into ledger with Merkle-root progress

sections.title: Sentinel Compliance Automation
capabilities: Policy-as-code (OPA/Rego) at CI/CD and runtime
Control catalogue mapped to NIST/ISO/EU AI Act/SR 11-7
Automated evidence assembly into bundles (EB-01…)
Scheduled + on-demand report generation (TR-01…TR-10)
Findings tracker with SLA-driven remediation workflow
Audit pack export (encrypted zip, sealed envelope, Merkle-root receipt)

sections.title: PID-Based Alignment Tuning
sections.description: A control-theoretic layer that measures alignment error e(t) between observed agent behaviour and declared specification, then modulates prompt, retrieval, and decoding parameters via a PID controller with auditable gains.

parameters.auditTrail: Every parameter update signed + stored in evidence ledger

m8_taxonomy.title: AI Safety Risk Taxonomy & Multi-Layered Governance
m8_taxonomy.summary: Nine-category risk taxonomy + six governance layers aligning strategic intent with operational control.

sections.title: Bias Detection & Mitigation Tools
tools: Demographic parity, equal opportunity, predictive parity metrics
Shapley-based feature attribution for disparate drivers
Continuous monitoring with PSI drift on protected slices

m9_incident.title: AI Safety Incident Response Playbooks
m9_incident.summary: Eight playbooks with RACI, SLAs, regulator notifications, and post-mortem templates.

sections.title: Playbook Anatomy
structure: Regulator notification (EU AI Act Art. 73: ≤72h) + templates
Root-cause analysis template (5 Whys + causal diagram)
Corrective & preventive actions with due dates

m10_backend.title: Backend Robustness, RBAC, Audit & Secure Gemini Integration
m10_backend.summary: Defensive engineering foundations: validation, error handling, RBAC/ABAC, audit, and secret-safe Gemini routing.

sections.title: Centralized Error Handling & Zod Validation
requirements: All request bodies, query params, and responses validated by Zod schemas
Central error middleware emits RFC 7807 Problem Details JSON
Errors categorised: validation, auth, policy, upstream, internal
Correlation ID (traceparent) propagated to client and logs
No stack traces leaked to clients; structured logs include stack server-side
Rate-limit and idempotency-key errors distinguished explicitly

sections.title: Secure Backend-Routed Gemini Integration
requirements: Gemini API key stored in KMS; never materialised in frontend
All LLM calls pass through signed backend envelopes
Request/response evidence stored with SHA3-256 hash in ledger
Safety settings enforced server-side (categories + thresholds)
Circuit breaker + exponential backoff + jittered retries
Quota manager per tenant with soft/hard caps

sections.title: RBAC + ABAC
controls: Policy: 'Governance may read all evidence; cannot modify templates'
Policy: 'IncidentResponder may trigger kill-switch with two-person rule'

sections.title: Audit Trails
requirements: Every state-changing action produces a signed audit record
Records chained via Merkle-DAG; daily root published to evidence portal
Viewer supports filter by actor, resource, action, outcome, time
Export to SIEM (Splunk, Chronicle, Sentinel)
WORM storage (S3 Object Lock) + 7-year retention default

sections.title: Signed Active Learning Loop
requirements: Human feedback captured with reviewer identity + rationale
Feedback payload signed with Ed25519 reviewer key
Replay capability: exact run + feedback reconstruction
Gemini fine-tuning / instruction ingestion only from signed feedback
Anti-spoofing: rate limits per reviewer + anomaly detector

m11_experience.title: Task Dependency DAG, Vision Analysis & PDF Export
m11_experience.summary: Visual and output-quality features that materially improve analyst productivity.

sections.title: Task Dependency DAG Visualisation (D3.js)
requirements: Live DAG rendering of agent task graphs
Causal lineage overlay (who called what, with evidence links)
Critical-path highlighting and bottleneck detection
Click-through to individual run evidence bundle
Large-graph virtualisation (>10k nodes) with focus+context lens
Export: SVG, PNG, PDF; shareable signed link

sections.title: Refined Vision Analysis Outputs
requirements: Structured output: objects, bounding boxes, OCR, rationale
Confidence calibration with temperature scaling report
PII auto-redaction with reversible mask (vault-backed)
Explicit uncertainty in ambiguous regions
Cross-check by a second vision model (ensemble vote)
Audit-log of all vision decisions with source artefact hash

sections.title: Advanced PDF Export Styling
requirements: Theme selector (Executive, Regulator, Internal, Accessible)
Header/footer with tenant logo, classification, page numbers
Watermark (dynamic: user, time, classification)
Table of contents and bookmarks auto-generated
Cover page with executive summary and key metrics
Appendix with signed evidence manifest & Merkle proof
PDF/A-3 option for long-term archival

activities: Roll out prompt lifecycle to all teams

sections.title: Change Management
activities: Executive sponsor readout at each phase gate
